stories.headline: Ships alter course in Strait of Hormuz amid US navy blockade
stories.summary: Tankers have stopped or turned back as Washington says more than a dozen warships are enforcing transit restrictions

stories.headline: Katrina Dudley on Oil Prices, Market Impact of War In Iran
stories.summary: Katrina Dudley, senior investment strategist at Franklin Templeton, discusses energy prices and market impacts of the war in Iran. She speaks with Matt Miller on "The Brief." (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Global oil demand to plunge amid disruptions caused by war on Iran: IEA
stories.summary: The IEA's oil 'demand destruction' report comes after its chief said unnamed countries are hoarding stocks.

stories.headline: Hamas rejects Gaza disarmament plan, Palestinian official says
stories.summary: The official tells the BBC that the armed group will not move forward with talks until Israel fully complies with its commitments.

stories.headline: IEA warns of historic oil supply shock as Iran war chokes global markets
stories.summary: The International Energy Agency (IEA) has warned of an unprecedented energy shock, with global oil production plunging by over 10 million barrels per day in March, as diplomatic efforts between Washington and Tehran fail.

By an overwhelming majority, they voted not only to oust a go

stories.headline: US blockade of Iran ports irresponsible and dangerous, China says
stories.summary: China, the biggest buyer of Iranian oil, says the US naval blockade undermines an "already fragile ceasefire".

stories.headline: Dow, Exxon and Rivals Are Raising Plastic Prices as Iran War Convulses Oil Market
stories.summary: Dow Inc. and Exxon Mobil Corp. are among the companies boosting prices for plastics as the sector grapples with supply shocks from the US-Israeli war on Iran.

stories.headline: US Says Six Ships U-Turned in First Day of Hormuz Blockade
stories.summary: The US said six merchant vessels complied with instructions from its forces to turn around and re-enter an Iranian port during the first day of its blockade, as traders watched for signs of ships testing the restrictions.

stories.headline: How much will US Hormuz blockade hurt Iran, and does Tehran have an escape?
stories.summary: Iran earned $5bn in oil exports in past month while it shut the strait for most other ships. That revenue is at risk.

stories.headline: How many ships are crossing the Strait of Hormuz?
stories.summary: Four vessels with links to Iran have crossed the Strait of Hormuz despite the US naval blockade.

stories.headline: Brazil's former spy chief detained by ICE agents in US
stories.summary: Alexandre Ramagem was sentenced to 16 years in jail for plotting a military coup in Brazil but had already fled the country.

stories.headline: IMF cuts UK’s growth forecast by more than any other G7 nation
stories.summary: Fund says British economy faces heavy blow from global energy crisis this year

stories.headline: Lagarde Says Early ECB Exit No Option With Clouds on Horizon
stories.summary: President Christine Lagarde said an early exit from the European Central Bank isn’t currently an option for her in the face of the economic crisis brought on by the Iran war.

stories.headline: Warsh Fed Chair Hearing Set for Next Week, Tim Scott Says
stories.summary: The Senate Banking Committee will hold a confirmation hearing next week for Kevin Warsh, President Donald Trump’s nominee for Federal Reserve chair, the panel’s top Republican said Tuesday.

stories.headline: Analyst Warns That Oil Prices Threaten Banks
stories.summary: JP Morgan’s earnings look strong, but the real story is beneath the surface says Baird Senior Research Analyst David George. He joined Bloomberg Open Interest to break down why net interest income fears are overblown, why trading gains won’t last, and the one macro risk investors should actually watch: rising oil prices and the ripple effect on consumers and corporate margins. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Eric Swalwell Says He Will Resign From Congress After Sex Abuse Accusations
stories.summary: The California lawmaker said in a social media post Monday that he would quit. He has denied accounts of abusing women.

stories.headline: Zelenskyy pitches new joint security system to European allies
stories.summary: Ukraine should be an integral part of Europe’s security, or some on the continent would be part of the "Russian world," Zelenskyy said as he announced discussions over a new joint defence system with EU and non-EU countries.

Accusers seek justice after unwanted explicit messages from Congressman Eric Swalwell
stories.summary: He denies claims of sexual assault but admits " mistakes" and says he will resign.

stories.headline: Watch: What are Harry and Meghan doing in Australia?

stories.headline: Founder of China's Evergrande pleads guilty to fraud
stories.summary: Evergrande was once China's biggest real estate firm, with a stock market valuation of more than $50bn.

stories.headline: JPMorgan and Citi post bumper profits as Iran war boosts traders
stories.summary: Jamie Dimon warns US economy faces ‘complex set of risks’ even as Wall Street banks benefit from market volatility

stories.headline: Gower Bullish on Middle East War Impact on Metals
stories.summary: Amy Gower, lead metals and mining strategist at Morgan Stanely, says the long-term effects of Middle East current events are more bullish for metals if they bring more stockpiling the long-term. She speaks on "Bloomberg Surveillance." (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Fiona Boal's Outlook for Markets, Hungary
stories.summary: S&P Dow Jones Indices Equities Global head Fiona Boal discusses markets surrounding the war in Iran and her outlook for Hungary. She speaks to Guy Johnson and Anna Edwards on "The Opening Trade." (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Equinor Halves Scatec Stake Amid Focus on Oil, Gas Output
stories.summary: Equinor ASA halved its stake in renewables developer Scatec ASA in a further realignment toward its core hydrocarbon business.

stories.headline: United CEO Proposes American Airlines Merger
stories.summary: A shocking airline mega-merger proposal could reshape global aviation. Bloomberg's Benedikt Kammel joined Bloomberg Open Interest to break down United’s bold pitch to merge with American Airlines, why crisis conditions are driving consolidation, and why US travelers pay far more than Europeans for flights. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: ECB's Lagarde on Iran War's Economic Impact, Rates and AI
stories.summary: European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde discusses the impact of the war in Iran and AI on the euro-zone economy with Bloomberg's Francine Lacqua at the IMF’s spring meetings. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Lagarde Says Europe’s Economy Has Slipped Below ECB Baseline
stories.summary: President Christine Lagarde said higher energy costs have pushed the euro zone away from the European Central Bank’s base-case outlook, though not enough yet to warrant leaning toward raising interest rates.

stories.headline: Trump and MAGA Embraced Orban. Now, They’re Worried by His Loss in Hungary’s Election.
stories.summary: Viktor Orban, the Hungarian prime minister, and his right-wing playbook were embraced by parts of the American right. Now some are worried by his defeat.

stories.headline: Tony Gonzales, a Texas Congressman, Says He Will Resign
stories.summary: The third-term Republican lawmaker is accused of coercing a staff member into a sexual relationship.

stories.headline: ‘We were never friends’: Kremlin plays down loss of ally following Orbán’s election defeat

stories.headline: Italy suspends defence agreement with Israel
stories.summary: Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni's government freezes automatic renewal of defence cooperation deal with Israel.

stories.headline: Bongbong Marcos fights ill-health rumours with star jumps
stories.summary: The Philippine President challenged anyone questioning his fitness to join him in the gym.

stories.headline: Spain approves plan to give around 500,000 undocumented migrants legal status
stories.summary: Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez said migrants helped "build the rich, open and diverse Spain that we are and to which we aspire".

stories.headline: Harry and Meghan mix charity and business on Australia visit
stories.summary: There are questions over whether local taxpayers are picking up any of the bill for policing the visit.

stories.headline: BlackRock profits jump as it draws in $130bn
stories.summary: Earnings for first quarter underline asset manager’s push into investment products that generate higher fees

stories.headline: UK migrant workforce grew strongly last year despite weaker hiring
stories.summary: Data highlights concerns about lack of jobs growth among British-born population

stories.headline: Citi Logs Best Returns in Five Years as Revamp Takes Hold
stories.summary: Citigroup Inc. traders rode a wave of volatility to push the Wall Street bank to its highest quarterly revenue in a decade, notching another success for Chief Executive Officer Jane Fraser’s turnaround plan.

stories.headline: Inside J&J’s Next Blockbuster Drug
stories.summary: J&J’s CFO Joseph Wolk joined Bloomberg Open Interest to talk about why its newly launched psoriasis pill could become one of its biggest drugs ever, already reaching 1,500 patients in just days. He explains how the company is avoiding risky mega-deals thanks to a strong pipeline, while confidently raising guidance and betting on long-term double-digit growth. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: U.S. Military Strikes Another Boat in Pacific, Killing 2
stories.summary: The attack was the 49th since the campaign against vessels in the Caribbean and the eastern Pacific started in early September.

stories.summary: Decision to reduce duty-free quotas by 47% aimed at curbing Chinese importsBusiness live – latest updatesThe EU is to go ahead with plans to double tariffs and halve quotas on imports of steel from July, in a move designed to curb Chinese imports but which could damage UK exports to the bloc.The decision by EU lawmakers and member states after late night talks on Monday, will reduce duty-free quotas by 47%. stories.headline: ‘We are a stable, predictable country’: Spanish PM Sanchez in Beijing
stories.summary: Spain's Pedro Sánchez and China's Xi Jinping stressed respect for international law and a shared “sense of justice”.

stories.headline: Champions League: Barcelona handball claim against Atletico Madrid rejected
stories.summary: Barca were unhappy with an alleged handball by Atletico's Marc Pubill in quarterfinal first leg but UEFA rejects appeal.

stories.headline: Tesla's Shanghai factory can build humanoid robots, China boss says

Nissan cuts model lineup by 20% as it unveils new AI tech strategy
Indian auto, textile production hit as gas crunch drives workers home
Singapore Q1 preliminary GDP up 4.6% on year, misses 5.9% forecast
Israel steps up strikes on Lebanon and Hezbollah attacks continue as talks loom
stories.summary: More than 160 people have been killed since Friday in Lebanon, where Israel is pressing on with its campaign against Hezbollah despite the US-Iran ceasefire.

stories.headline: What the U.S. Blockade of Iran and the Strait of Hormuz Might Look Like
stories.summary: The U.S. military has provided few details on how it might carry out President Trump’s orders as he seeks to pressure Tehran on a peace deal. But history and established practices offer some clues.

stories.headline: As Iran war drags on, midterm forecasts for Republicans get even worse
stories.summary: The GOP was already bracing for a tough election, but with the ceasefire in doubt, gas prices are likely to stay high, and voters’ economic outlook is dismal.

stories.headline: Eric Swalwell to resign from Congress after sexual misconduct claims
stories.summary: Four women have accused the congressman and ex-candidate for California governor of misconduct ranging from sexual harassment to rape, which he has denied.

stories.headline: The US blockade of Iran is a gamble. Will it work?
stories.summary: The US military is gambling on the blockade denying Iran vital trade - but questions remain on whether the bet will pay off.

stories.headline: Hungary's next PM would pick up if Putin calls and tell him to stop Ukraine war
stories.summary: Russia has offered "pragmatic" relations, but Péter Magyar says he will not call Vladimir Putin himself.

stories.headline: Orbán era swept away by Péter Magyar's Hungary election landslide
stories.summary: Viktor Orbán's 16-year rule is over, defeated by a 45-year-old ex-party insider who convinced a majority of Hungarians to oust him.

stories.headline: Stocks Climb, Oil Tumbles on Iran Peace Push Hopes: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: US stocks extended gains, with the Nasdaq 100 on pace for its longest winning streak since 2021, as optimism on the possibility of another round of peace talks between the US and Iran pushed down oil prices and boosted risk appetite around the globe.

stories.headline: Italian PM condemns ally Trump over 'unacceptable' Pope criticism
stories.summary: Italy's prime minister and the US president are close allies, but Trump has refused to apologise to the "very weak" Pope Leo XIV.

stories.headline: Ex-Nigerian oil minister denies taking bribes
stories.summary: Diezani Alison-Madueke is accused of living “a life of luxury” in the UK at properties paid for and refurbished by industry figures.

stories.headline: Philippines accuses Chinese fishermen of dumping cyanide in South China Sea
stories.summary: Beijing rejects the allegation as a "farce", while Manila claims the poison was used to kill fish and deprive troops stationed in the area of food.

stories.headline: Ukraine and Russia accuse each other of hundreds of ceasefire violations
stories.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky says his nation's forces would respond "symmetrically" to Russian attacks.

stories.headline: Hungary’s Viktor Orban, ally of Trump and Putin, concedes election defeat
stories.summary: WIth record turnout, Hungarians chose to end the 16-year rule of the prime minister who was a self-proclaimed champion of illiberal Christian democracy.

stories.headline: Political turmoil in Indian border state as nine million lose voting rights
stories.summary: Nine million voters have been dropped from rolls in West Bengal, raising concerns over exclusion and fairness.

stories.headline: Lebanon thought there was a ceasefire - then Israel unleashed deadly blitz
stories.summary: Israel says Lebanon is not included in the ceasefire the US agreed to halt the war with Iran.

stories.headline: Hot in the city: Energy crisis tests Singapore's air-con addiction
stories.summary: The rise in energy prices has hit Asia particularly hard as many nations are heavily reliant on Gulf oil.

stories.headline: How war has made a 33-year-old the Czech Republic’s richest man
stories.summary: The rapid rise of CSG, Europe’s newest defence giant

stories.headline: Argentina passes bill loosening protection of its glaciers
stories.summary: The new bill will make it easier for provincial governments to allow mining projects in glacier regions.

stories.headline: Iran ceasefire deal gives Trump a way out of war - but at a high cost
stories.summary: The path to the two-week ceasefire with Iran may have fundamentally altered the way the rest of the world views the US.

stories.headline: How Pakistan helped secure a fragile ceasefire between the US and Iran
stories.summary: Pakistan has a historic relationship with Iran, but an agreement was far from certain, writes the BBC’s Caroline Davies

stories.headline: Why Real Madrid tie could make or break Kane's Ballon d'Or hopes
stories.summary: Harry Kane is the leading goalscorer in Europe but does he need to win a major trophy to have a chance of winning the prestigious award?

stories.headline: The US refinery now processing Venezuelan oil
stories.summary: Chevron is now importing 250,000 barrels of crude per day from Venezuela.

stories.headline: Carnival fever hits Lagos as locals celebrate Afro-Brazilian heritage
stories.summary: The centuries-old Fanti Carnival returned to Nigeria on Monday with a burst of colour and spectacle.
